description: Edge: ALL cards cost 2+ AP with only 3 AP — AP crunch test

hand.name: Double Strike
hand.description: Next attack card hits twice. Charge bonus: also pierces. Costs 2 AP.

hand.name: Slow
hand.description: Skip enemy's next defend or buff. Costs 2 AP.

hand.name: Entrench
hand.description: Double current block. Costs 2 AP. (block is 0, so useless here)

notes: EDGE CASE: Every card costs 2 AP. Player has only 3 AP. Can play exactly 1 card per turn. Fortify is dead (block=0). Double Strike without a follow-up attack is wasted (no AP left). Double Strike + Heavy Strike = 5 AP (can't do). Heavy Strike (2ap) is the most direct option but doesn't kill (10 HP, block 6, only 10+bonus=13 QP with Whetstone... wait, no Whetstone here). Does Slow prevent the incoming 6 damage? Yes, if it cancels a defend/buff — but enemy intends ATTACK, so Slow may be irrelevant? This tests whether agents understand Slow only cancels defend/buff intents.
